semanticCore: Earning, acquiring, committing crime, peel, bark, or accusation
quranicRelevance: Committing sins/earning evil (Iqtarafa - 'wa man yaqtarif hasanatan', 'wa liyartaqiboo ma hum muqtarifoon').

keyDerivations.meaning: To earn, acquire, commit (sin), take on, or gain

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَأَمْوَالٌ اقْتَرَفْتُمُوهَا (9:24): And wealth you have acquired/earned (business investments that must not delay migration).
وَمَنْ يَقْتَرِفْ حَسَنَةً نَزِدْ لَهُ فِيهَا حُسْنًا (42:23): And whoever earns/commits a good deed, We increase for him therein goodness.
discardedNoise: Disgust and revulsion (Qaraf) in modern colloquial Arabic.
Barks of cinnamon trees (Qurfah) in spice markets.
summaryEn: The root denotes earning and acquisition (Iqtiraf), balancing the warning against hoarding earned cash with the promise to enrich those who earn good deeds.
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على الاكتساب والملابسة، جامعاً بين كسب وصناعة العمل الصالح (يقترف حسنة)، والاكتساب المالي الدنيوي، واقتراف الآثام.
rawText: أَعْرَضَتِ القِرْفَةُ signifies إِتَّسَعَتْ: (TA, art. عرض:) and اِتَّسَعَتْ قِرْفَتُهُ signifies كَثُرَ مَنْ يَتَّهِمُهُ. (TA, art. لبس.) See voce عَرُضَ. قَارَفَهُ 3 He was, or became, near to it; meaning some base thing, or the like. (TA.) See قَرَفٌ. قَرَفٌ The mixing with others; [and particularly with others who are diseased or the like]; a subst. from 🡻مُقَارَفَةٌ: (K:) the being near to [a person, or persons, or a place, infected with] disease: (S, TA:) the being near to pestilence, or epidemic disease. (T in art. تلف.) See تَلَفٌ. مَقْرِفٌ A place of paring off: see an ex. voce صَمْغٌ.
